title: Shanks
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Shanks.jpg]] |name=Shanks |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], [[Feswe Corridor]][[Star Wars (1977) 34|''Star Wars'' (1977) 34]] |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]][[Star Wars (1977) 25|''Star Wars'' (1977) 25]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Blond]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin=Light |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] *[[House of Tagge/Legends|House of Tagge]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I'm proud to be the military aide on this experiment, sir."
"And fortunate, Shanks! For when it's over, Darth Vader and his wizard's ways won't hold the Emperor's fancy... my family will!|Shanks and Orman Tagge|}} '''Shanks''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as a [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Shanks served as a military liaison between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[House of Tagge/Legends|House of Tagge]] in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], working directly under [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Orman Tagge/Legends|Orman Tagge]]. The lieutenant served as a key advisor to Tagge during the [[Yavin blockade]] and later on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], where the Tagges planned to annihilate the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] with a [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] called the [[Omega Frost/Legends|Omega Frost]]. However, the Rebel [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] foiled the Tagges' plot, and Shanks was [[Death/Legends|killed]] when the Rebels destroyed the Tagge [[Mining Explorer|flagship]]. ==Biography== {{Quote|You're too skillful, Baron Tagge! Only a Jedi Knight could equal you now—and they no longer exist!|Shanks and Baron Tagge, during lightsaber training|}} A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Shanks progressed to the rank of [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] by [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]]—the wake of the Empire's stunning defeat at the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]—the Imperial-allied [[House of Tagge/Legends|House of Tagge]] was enlisted to aid in the [[Yavin blockade]], where the Tagges planned to use an enormous turbine called [[Achtnak Turbine Station]] to hide constant [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighter]] runs on the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Great Temple/Legends|base]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. Shanks was assigned as the Tagges' Imperial military aide on the mission, working under [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Orman Tagge/Legends|Orman Tagge]] aboard a Tagge [[Mining Explorer]]. Shanks' duties included assisting in Tagge's [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] training, as the man fancied himself a master of the ancient weapon of the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]]. Tagge, who was preparing for a long-desired showdown with the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], was frequently dissatisfied with the quality of Shanks' opposition. [[File:Ormanduel.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Shanks training with Orman Tagge.]] At first, Shanks and Tagge deployed Achtnak Turbine Station to devastating effect, allowing them to hide their [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] within the [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin Prime]] before springing bombing runs out of seemingly nowhere. However, Shanks expressed concerns that Tagge should have moved the station deeper within Yavin Prime, fears that proved validated when Rebel [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] figured out the turbine's location and bombed it to smithereens. Shanks and Tagge narrowly managed to escape, retreating to the Mining Explorer and surreptitiously following Skywalker's path out of Yavin Prime.[[Star Wars (1977) 26|''Star Wars'' (1977) 26]] Afterward, Shanks accompanied Tagge and his brother, [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Silas Tagge/Legends|Silas Tagge]], in a mission to [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], where the Tagges were testing another new weapon: the [[Omega Frost/Legends|Omega Frost]], a [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] with the power to freeze any object containing [[Water/Legends|moisture]]. Orman and Silas planned to lure the Rebel [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] into a trap in the [[Feswe Corridor]] and annihilate them with the Omega Frost, and Shanks served as a liaison between them and a third Tagge brother—Imperial [[General/Legends|General]] [[Ulric Tagge/Legends|Ulric Tagge]]—who had a battle group on standby.[[Star Wars (1977) 33|''Star Wars'' (1977) 33]] However, Skywalker once again got involved in their plans, boarding the Tagges' ship and besting Orman in a lightsaber [[Duel aboard the Tagge Mining Explorer|duel]] that left the Baron incapacitated in the Mining Explorer's [[Medical bay/Legends|sick bay]]. With Orman in clinical shock, Shanks served directly under Silas in the [[Battle of the Corridor|battle to come]]. Although Silas planned to go ahead with their plan even without Orman, Shanks feared further meddling from Skywalker, and once again his misgivings proved well-founded. After fleeing Tagge pursuit in a stolen TIE fighter, the young Rebel managed to reach one of the Omega Frost's conductors, rendering the device inoperable. Silas fled the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] in a panic after seeing his plans coming apart, leaving Shanks to command the sitting-duck Mining Explorer. The Rebels soon swept in and destroyed the ship, killing almost everyone aboard.[[Star Wars (1977) 35|''Star Wars'' (1977) 35]] ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|To think I felt being military liaison officer for this bunch would further my career! Why did I—?|Shanks|}} An ambitious young Imperial lieutenant, Shanks was eager to work with the Tagges, seeing it as a perfect opportunity to advance himself in the Imperial hierarchy. However, while Shanks was frequently impressed with the intelligence and acumen of Orman and Silas Tagge, their cavalier plans had a tendency to make him nervous. By the time of the battle in the Feswe Corridor, Shanks had grown to regret ever thinking working with the Tagges could further his career. Shanks trained extensively with a lightsaber during his time with Orman Tagge, although his skills were not quite up to the Baron's muster. ==Behind the scenes== Shanks first appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 25|''Star Wars'' (1977) 25]], written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and released in [[1979]]. Shanks would later go on to appear in three more issues in the original [[Marvel Comics]] [[Star Wars (1977)|''Star Wars'']] comic run—in all four of his appearances, he was illustrated by [[Carmine Infantino]]. ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars (1977) 25|''Star Wars'' (1977) 25]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 25}} {{1st}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 26|''Star Wars'' (1977) 26]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 26}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 33|''Star Wars'' (1977) 33]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 33}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 34|''Star Wars'' (1977) 34]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 34}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial Navy officers of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Males]]
